scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE Civil Judge (Senior Division)
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute this suit, and the Defendant is a person sui juris and can be sued.
2. The Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, and the cause of action arose within Bengaluru, thereby invoking the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.
3. This suit is filed in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it, and the suit value is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court under Section 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is filed within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which applies to money payable for money lent. The suit is within the prescribed period from the date of default.

FACTS OF THE TRANSACTION

5. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant as a hand loan via NEFT bank transfer from the Plaintiff's bank account to the Defendant's bank account.
6. The transaction is evidenced by the bank transaction record bearing UTR Number AXIB20240315123456, which is annexed hereto as Annexure A.
7. The Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through digital communications via WhatsApp messages, which are annexed hereto as Annexure B.

DEFAULT AND DEMAND

8. The repayment of the loan amount was due on demand, and the Plaintiff made repeated oral demands for repayment towards the Defendant.
9. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to the Defendant demanding repayment of the outstanding loan amount, which is annexed hereto as Annexure C.
10. Despite the expiry of the legal notice and the demands made, the Defendant has failed and neglected to repay the loan amount or any part thereof to the Plaintiff.

QUANTIFICATION OF CLAIM

11. The principal amount due and payable by the Defendant to the Plaintiff is Rs. 15,00,000 (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only).
12. The Plaintiff claims interest at the rate of 12% per annum on the principal amount from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 till the date of realization, calculated as simple interest.
13. The total outstanding claim as on the date of filing this plaint comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000 along with the accrued interest calculated at the aforementioned rate.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the money lent under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the Defendant has committed a breach of the obligation to repay the loan.
15. The plea of no consideration is pre-empted as the mode of payment is established through the documentary trail of the NEFT transfer with the specific UTR number.
16. The claim is within limitation as the Defendant acknowledged the debt via WhatsApp messages, and the suit is filed within the prescribed period from the date of default and legal notice.
17. The interest rate claimed is within reasonable market norms for unsecured lending and is not usurious, warranting compensation for the wrongful withholding of money.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. The cause of action first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant failed to repay the loan amount despite the demand, and subsequently upon the expiry of the legal notice dated 01.07.2024.
19. The cause of action arose within the jurisdiction of this Court and the suit is filed within the limitation period prescribed by law.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}} for the purpose of jurisdiction and court fee under the applicable Court Fees Act.
21. The requisite court fee of {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} has been paid on the plaint, and the plaint is properly stamped and tendered with the requisite court fee.

INTEREST

22. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows:

    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{DATE_OF_DEFAULT}} (date of default) until the date of institution of the suit;
    (b) Pendente lite interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of institution of the suit until the date of decree, under Section 34 CPC;
    (c) Post-decree interest at such rate as the Court deems fit and just from the date of decree until realization of the entire decretal amount, under Section 34 CPC.

PRAYER

23. In the premises aforesaid,
